// Negative fixture for spec 120 R6 scan (g), fail-closed command
// resolution (round 11): the bd command name hides in a LOCAL variable
// (program := "bd"), and a second spawn's command operand is fully
// dynamic. BOTH spawns must surface as sites requiring classification —
// the local assignment via sound single-assignment propagation, the
// dynamic one via the fail-closed default (an operand that cannot be
// PROVEN non-bd/non-git is never silently skipped).
package fixture

import (
	"os"
	"os/exec"
)

func localVarCmd(id string) ([]byte, error) {
	program := "bd" // local single assignment — must resolve to bd
	return exec.Command(program, "show", id).Output()
}

func dynamicCmd(id string) ([]byte, error) {
	tool := os.Getenv("MINDSPEC_FIXTURE_TOOL") // unresolvable — fail closed
	return exec.Command(tool, "show", id).Output()
}
